title: T-092 — Shared BOM SSOT schema for NPD Builder initial BOM + Technical approval lifecycle
prompt:
# T-092 — shared BOM SSOT schema for NPD Builder initial BOM

PRD: 01-NPD-PRD.md 2026-05-03 FINAL Amendment + _meta/decisions/2026-05-03-flow-d365-settings-technical-decisions.md
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Final decisions this task must implement
- `FG` / Finished Good is canonical; `FA` is compatibility alias only.
- Stage-Gate G0-G4 is canonical MVP. Brief creates NPD project (`DEV-123`); project creates/maps FG at G3.
- NPD Builder creates WIP/intermediate items + FG + the initial shared BOM/product-spec version after all required department closures and approval.
- One shared BOM table/model is SSOT across NPD, Technical, Planning, Production and integrations.
- D365 is export/import integration only and must not be schema source of truth.
- Released NPD product/BOM/spec edits create a new version and require Technical approval before factory use.

## Goal
Extend the existing shared BOM schema owned with Technical so NPD Builder can create the initial BOM/product-spec version in the same canonical `bom_headers`/`bom_lines` model used by Technical, Planning and Production. This is now ACTIONABLE under the final 2026-05-03 decisions; do not keep this task blocked on FA/FG or BOM SSOT clarification.

## Implementation contract
1. Inspect existing `bom_headers` and `bom_lines` schema/migrations before editing. Reuse the existing shared Technical BOM model where possible; do not create an NPD-only BOM table.
2. Ensure `bom_headers` can represent an NPD-originated initial version:
   - status/lifecycle supports at least `draft`, `in_review`, `technical_approved`, `active`, `superseded`, `archived` (map to existing enum names if already equivalent; document mapping in comments/tests).
   - `origin_module` enum/string supports `npd`, `technical`, `imported` and defaults safely for existing rows.
   - link to NPD project/legacy FA compatibility identifier for traceability (prefer `npd_project_id`; keep nullable legacy `fa_id`/`fa_code` only if already present or needed by existing code).
   - link to released `product_id`/FG once NPD Builder creates/maps the FG.
   - add constraints so a header cannot be orphaned: it must reference an FG/product or an NPD project/compatibility FA during pre-release.
3. Add version fields or verify existing fields support immutable versioning (`version`, `supersedes_bom_header_id`, `effective_from`, approval timestamps/users). Do not mutate approved active versions in place.
4. Add indexes for NPD project lookup, FG/product lookup, active version lookup and Technical approval queue lookup.
5. Add schema comments/JSDoc stating `bom_headers/bom_lines` are shared BOM SSOT and D365 is integration only.
6. Mark any legacy NPD computed BOM view as DEPRECATED/preview-only; do not drop it in this task. Reader switch/backfill/drop is T-093.
7. Migration filename: `0092_shared_bom_ssot_npd_origin.sql` or next available sequence if numbering differs.

## Acceptance criteria
- Given schema introspection after migration, when `bom_headers` is inspected, then it can store NPD-originated initial BOM versions and Technical-originated versions in the same table/model.
- Given an NPD project before FG release, when inserting an NPD-originated draft/in_review BOM header linked to that project/compatibility FA and no product_id yet, then insert succeeds.
- Given an orphan BOM header with neither product/FG nor NPD project/compatibility FA link, when inserted, then it fails.
- Given a released/active BOM version exists, when a change is requested, then schema supports creating a new version/superseding relationship rather than updating the active version in place.
- Given Technical approval fields/status are inspected, when a new post-release version is created, then it can remain non-active until Technical approval.
- Given code comments/schema docs are grepped, then they state shared BOM is SSOT and D365 is not source of truth.
- Given the legacy computed NPD BOM view remains, then it is clearly marked DEPRECATED/preview-only and not described as canonical.

## Test strategy
- RED schema tests for lifecycle enum/status mapping, origin_module, orphan constraint, NPD pre-release insert, released-version supersession, and Technical approval pending state.
- Migration dry-run on fresh DB and existing fixture DB.
- Run relevant db/schema tests plus `pnpm tsc --noEmit` if available.

## Risk red lines
- Do not create a second NPD BOM SSOT table.
- Do not treat D365 import/cache tables as canonical BOM/product source.
- Do not drop the legacy computed view in this task; only deprecate/comment it.
- Do not mutate approved active BOM/product-spec versions in place.
- Do not remove FA compatibility aliases/routes/views in this task.
- Do not run in parallel with T-093 backfill/drop or any broad terminology refactor.

pipeline_inputs.description: ACTIONABLE final-decision task: extend shared bom_headers/bom_lines so NPD Builder can create initial FG/WIP BOM version in the shared BOM SSOT with Technical approval/version lifecycle.
pipeline_inputs.details: Implements 2026-05-03 final decisions: FG canonical, FA compatibility alias only, Stage-Gate G0-G4, NPD Builder initial WIP+FG+BOM/product-spec version, shared BOM SSOT, D365 integration only, post-release edits as new version requiring Technical approval.
scope_files: Existing shared BOM schema/migrations/tests only; exact paths must be discovered before editing
Legacy NPD computed BOM view comments only (deprecate/preview-only; do not drop)
out_of_scope: Changing bom_lines shape unless existing schema cannot represent initial NPD BOM lines
acceptance_criteria: Given schema introspection after migration, when bom_headers is inspected, then it can store NPD-originated initial BOM versions and Technical-originated versions in the same shared model.
Given an NPD project before FG release, when inserting an NPD-originated draft/in_review BOM linked to that project/compatibility FA with product_id NULL, then insert succeeds.
Given an orphan BOM header with neither product/FG nor NPD project/compatibility FA link, when inserted, then it fails.
Given a released/active BOM exists, when a change is requested, then schema supports creating a new version/superseding relationship rather than mutating the active version in place.
Given a post-release new version exists, when status is not Technical-approved, then it cannot be treated as active/factory-approved.
Given docs/comments are grepped, then shared BOM is documented as SSOT and D365 as integration/export only.
Given the legacy computed NPD BOM view remains, then it is marked DEPRECATED/preview-only and not canonical.
test_strategy: Update RED schema test to cover origin_module, fa_id, CHECK constraint, lifecycle enum values.
Run `pnpm vitest run` + `pnpm drizzle-kit generate` + `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.
risk_red_lines: Do not create an NPD-only BOM SSOT table.
Do not treat D365 as source of truth; it is integration/export/import only.
Do not drop the legacy computed view in this task.
Do not mutate approved active BOM/product-spec versions in place.
Do not remove FA compatibility aliases in this task.
Do not run in parallel with T-093 backfill/drop or broad terminology refactor.
